700 ÛÒ 712 Main Street commonly known as the Old City Hall Building, is situated at the heart of Historic Downtown Martinez. The property was one of the first downtown buildings in the last half of the 20th century to be completely renovated in a way that keeps it's early 20th century style and architectural features. This 14,000 SF building consists of Retail and Office on the ground floor, and Office on the second floor. Built during the refinery boom in 1914, it was the first apartment building in Martinez. The property sits astride the Alhambra Creek which meanders through the plaza next to the building. The realignment of the creek in 2000 sparked a complete seismic retrofit of the property in 2001. The current rent roll offers flexibility for an owner user or an investor wishing to create value by a combination of lease renewals and vacancy absorption.

space_description: Prime epicenter location in SF's historic Fisherman's Wharf. The wharf's rich history and authenticity has attracted both locals (35% of visitors) and tourists for over a century. From it's roots as a working Fisherman's Market and industrial Center to today's mix of fresh seafood, fine beverages, entertainment, shopping, views and sightseeing. Fisherman's Wharf continues to be San Francisco's most visited attraction. Originally a chicken feed warehouse, the building was completely redeveloped in 2000 to a 100,000 square foot project complex for both locals and visitors. Featured: Madame Tussauds Wax Museum and SF Dungeon. Homegrown Boudin SF is across the street. It includes a cafe, working bakery, and restaurant making it one of San Francisco's top grossing venues.
